diagrams.description: Human Geography is the study of the spatial organization of human activities and the relationships between people and their environments. This flowchart illustrates its analytical framework: the overarching goal is to understand and explain spatial patterns of human behavior, culture, and society. It draws on core concepts (space, place, scale, region, landscape) and subfields (cultural, economic, urban, political, social geography). These are investigated through diverse methods (GIS, cartography, qualitative fieldwork, quantitative spatial analysis) and framed by major theoretical perspectives (positivism, humanistic geography, Marxist and feminist geography, post‑structuralism). The resulting analysis addresses key issues like globalization, migration, urban inequality, and environmental perception, and informs applications in planning, policy, and sustainable development.
